title: What has carrying this resentment cost me?
subtitle: A journal prompt for making the cost of a long-held grievance visible
excerpt: A journal prompt for the resentment that has been in the room for a long time. Not whether to forgive. What the holding has actually cost, in time, attention, and the version of yourself that might exist without it.

practice.whenToUse: When a long-held resentment is worth examining — not to force forgiveness, but to see clearly what the holding has cost
practice.whenNotToUse: In very raw or recent hurt, where this kind of accounting is premature

content.text: A journal prompt for taking a clear accounting of a long-held resentment. Not to judge yourself for holding it. To see what it has cost and what you might do with that information. Take twenty minutes.
Name the resentment specifically. What happened? Who was involved? How long have you been carrying it?
What does it feel like in the body when you think about it? Where does it sit?
How much mental space has this resentment occupied, in the shower, in the car, when you're trying to sleep? What else might have lived in that space?
Has holding this resentment changed how you behave, with the person involved or with others? What has that cost?
What would it feel like to put this down, without agreeing it was fine, and stop carrying the weight of it? What does the person who has put it down look like?
